title: Agent Pipeline Configuration
description: Per-project configuration for the agent-pipeline plugin. Lives at .pipeline/config.json in the host repo.

repo.description: GitHub repo slug, e.g. 'owner/name'. Agents only act on PRs in this repo.

ghUser.description: GitHub username whose PRs the pipeline will act on. Other authors (Dependabot, bots, contributors) are skipped.

backend.description: Where tickets live. 'linear' uses the user's Linear MCP integration; 'filesystem' uses .pipeline/queue/ as a local ticket store.

projectFilter.description: Optional project name or ID to scope tickets.

queueDir.description: Directory where ticket JSON files live. State is encoded by subdirectory (needs-work, in-progress, done, etc.).

labelNamespace.description: Prefix for pipeline state labels. Default: 'pipeline:needs-work', 'pipeline:ready-for-human', etc.

agentLabelNamespace.description: Prefix for agent provenance labels. Default: 'agent:worker', 'agent:tester', etc.

verify.description: Shell commands the worker runs to verify a change before opening a PR. Stop here — agents must NOT run tests (orphaned process risk).

branchPrefix.description: Prefix for branches the worker creates.

worktreeRoot.description: Directory (relative to repo root) where the worker creates isolated git worktrees.

lessonsDir.description: Where the orchestrator and transcript-reviewer write self-audit lessons that compound across cycles.

transcriptsDir.description: Directory of raw Claude Code session transcripts (*.jsonl) the transcript-reviewer reads. Defaults to this repo's Claude Code projects dir. Reviewed read-only — raw content is never copied into tickets/PRs.

cadence.description: Number of completed runs to accumulate before the orchestrator dispatches transcript-reviewer (it also runs after any human intervention).

rulesDir.description: Directory of project-specific rule files (markdown) the scanner reads. Empty = scanner only checks generic categories.

glossaryPath.description: Path to the project glossary the glossary-maintainer keeps current.

readyQueueSaturation.description: When 'ready-for-human' has this many open PRs, the orchestrator stops dispatching detectors — the human is the bottleneck.

humanReviewer.description: GitHub handle of the human who reviews and merges PRs. The pipeline routes 'ready-for-human' PRs to this reviewer.

relevance.description: Tuning for the relevance-checker agent. Absent ⇒ feature off; existing installs are unaffected.

enabled.description: Master switch. When false (or the whole object is absent), the orchestrator never dispatches relevance-checker.

ticketStaleHours.description: A needs-work ticket older than this (by mtime) becomes eligible for a relevance check.

prStaleHours.description: A ready-for-human item older than this becomes eligible for a relevance check.

autoResolveConfidence.description: Minimum verdict confidence at which an 'obsolete' item is auto-resolved (moved to obsolete/ / PR closed) rather than flagged for a human.

autoClosePRs.description: GitHub backend: run `gh pr close` on a high-confidence-obsolete PR (reversible). When false, only label/flag it.

detectors.description: Per-rule detector fleet (detectors.registry.json) + diff-gate tuning. Absent ⇒ defaults apply.

registryPath.description: Path to the detector registry (single source of truth for the per-rule detector agents).

fullSweepEveryNCycles.description: Every N cycles the orchestrator ignores the changed-files cursor and runs all sweep/both detectors across src/, backstopping cursor gaps.

diffGate.description: The needs-detector-gate stage that runs matched diff-mode detectors on a PR's changed lines.

enabled.description: When false, code-reviewer advances straight past needs-detector-gate with no detector panel.

maxAutoFixDiffLines.description: Soft ceiling on changed lines for an auto-fix PR. The worker splits or flags `needs-split` rather than open a larger PR, keeping each PR tiny and revertible.
